amenities.name: Lakeside Clubhouse
amenities.description: Primary community clubhouse featuring a great room, kitchenette, and outdoor patio overlooking the pond.
amenities.rules: Reservations required for private events. Noise must be kept to a reasonable level after 9:00 PM. No tape or nails on walls. Resident must be present for the duration of the event.
amenities.location: Adjacent to the main entrance roundabout, next to the leasing office

amenities.name: Community Pool
amenities.description: Outdoor pool with lap lanes, shallow play area, and shaded lounge chairs.
amenities.rules: Pool hours are 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM. No lifeguard on duty—swim at your own risk. Children under 14 must be accompanied by an adult.
amenities.location: Behind Buildings C and D, accessible via the east pathway

amenities.name: North Playground
amenities.description: Children’s playground with swings, slides, and climbing structures. Includes benches and a small shaded area.
amenities.rules: Playground closes at dusk. Children must be supervised by an adult. No glass containers inside the play area.

amenities.name: East Tennis Courts
amenities.description: Two lighted tennis courts available for resident use and reserved play.
amenities.rules: Courts may be reserved in 1-hour blocks. Appropriate non-marking shoes required. No food or pets on the courts.
amenities.location: East side of the community, past the mail kiosk

amenities.name: Fitness Center
amenities.description: 24-hour access fitness room with cardio equipment, free weights, and stretching area.
amenities.rules: Residents only. Wipe down equipment after use. No dropping weights. Guests must be signed in at the office.
amenities.location: Lower level of the Lakeside Clubhouse

elections.name: 2026 HOA Board General Election
elections.description: Annual election for three open Board of Directors seats.
elections.instructions: You may select up to 3 candidates. Review each candidate’s profile before casting your vote. Once submitted, your ballot cannot be changed.

elections.name: 2026 Budget Ratification Vote
elections.description: Up-or-down vote on the proposed 2026 operating budget.
elections.instructions: You may select either APPROVE or REJECT for the proposed budget. A simple majority of votes cast is required for ratification.

elections.name: 2025 Special Board Election
elections.description: Special election held to fill one vacant Board seat.
elections.instructions: This election has ended. Results are posted under Meeting Minutes.

forum_categories.name: Lost and Found
forum_categories.description: Post about lost or found items within the community, such as keys, pets, and packages.

forum_categories.name: General Discussion
forum_categories.description: Open forum for community news, questions, and conversations not covered by other categories.

forum_categories.name: Events & Social
forum_categories.description: Discuss upcoming community events, carpooling, and social activities.

forum_categories.name: Maintenance Feedback
forum_categories.description: Share feedback and suggestions related to maintenance and common areas.

maintenance_requests.description: Loose handrail on the small slide platform. Kids are using it to pull themselves up and it moves noticeably.
maintenance_requests.last_status_update_note: Vendor scheduled to tighten and replace bolts on handrail this week.

maintenance_requests.location: Guest parking lot near main entrance
maintenance_requests.description: Several faded guest parking space lines need to be repainted. It is difficult to see the boundaries at night.
Hairline cracks in the stucco along the stairwell wall. No water intrusion reported yet.
maintenance_requests.last_status_update_note: Inspection completed. Repair to be bundled with upcoming exterior paint project.

maintenance_requests.description: Two sprinkler heads appear to be leaking overnight, causing pooling water and muddy areas along the walking path.
maintenance_requests.last_status_update_note: Sprinkler heads replaced and timer adjusted. Monitor for further pooling.

maintenance_requests.description: Right-most treadmill suddenly powers off during use and displays an error code.

pre_approved_colors.name: Neutral Beige
pre_approved_colors.description: Soft beige tone approved for primary exterior surfaces. Recommended for sun-facing elevations.

pre_approved_colors.name: Neutral Sandstone
pre_approved_colors.description: Warm sandstone hue suitable for stucco exteriors and trim.

pre_approved_colors.name: Neutral Warm Gray
pre_approved_colors.description: Balanced warm gray that pairs well with white or dark trim.

pre_approved_colors.name: Neutral Soft Taupe
pre_approved_colors.description: Soft taupe with subtle brown undertones. Popular for two-story homes.

pre_approved_colors.name: Olive Grove
pre_approved_colors.description: Muted olive green that blends with mature landscaping.

pre_approved_colors.name: Sienna Clay
pre_approved_colors.description: Rich clay-inspired tone approved for accent walls and columns.

pre_approved_colors.name: Slate Green
pre_approved_colors.description: Deep slate green recommended for lower levels or accent sections.

pre_approved_colors.name: Harbor Navy
pre_approved_colors.description: Bold navy blue approved only for shutters and small accent areas.

pre_approved_colors.name: Urban Charcoal
pre_approved_colors.description: Dark charcoal suitable for doors and metal railings.

pre_approved_colors.name: Heritage Red
pre_approved_colors.description: Classic deep red used sparingly on front doors only.

pre_approved_colors.name: Crisp White Trim
pre_approved_colors.description: Bright white trim color that coordinates with all approved neutrals.

pre_approved_colors.name: Soft Cream Trim
pre_approved_colors.description: Warm off-white recommended for fascia and window trim.

pre_approved_colors.name: Blue Gray Accent
pre_approved_colors.description: Cool blue-gray accent color suitable for shutters and doors.

pre_approved_colors.name: Forest Accent
pre_approved_colors.description: Deep forest green accent for doors and decorative trim only.

pre_approved_colors.name: Cocoa Accent
pre_approved_colors.description: Medium-dark cocoa brown accent color pairs well with beige exteriors.

pre_approved_colors.name: HOA Custom Match
pre_approved_colors.description: Use for HOA-approved custom color matches. Requires sample and board review.

rules.title: No Parking in Fire Lanes
rules.description: Vehicles may not park, stop, or stand in designated fire lanes at any time. Violations may result in immediate towing at owner’s expense.

rules.title: Guest Parking Time Limit
rules.description: Guest spaces are limited to 48 consecutive hours per vehicle without written approval from the HOA.

rules.title: Quiet Hours
rules.description: Quiet hours are from 10:00 PM to 7:00 AM daily. During these times, residents must keep noise to a level that cannot be heard inside neighboring units.

rules.title: Repeated Noise Violations
rules.description: Second and subsequent violations of the quiet hours policy within a 6-month period incur higher fines.

rules.title: Unauthorized Exterior Modifications
rules.description: Any exterior modification (including paint color changes, structural additions, or window replacements) performed without prior written architectural approval is prohibited.

rules.title: Trash Bin Storage
rules.description: Trash and recycling bins must be stored out of public view except on collection days.

rules.title: Clubhouse Private Event Rules
rules.description: Private events must be reserved in advance and may not exceed posted capacity. Alcohol consumption must comply with local laws.

rules.title: Pool Guest Limit
rules.description: Each household may bring up to 4 guests to the pool at a time. Residents must accompany guests at all times.

rules.title: Pet Leash Requirement
rules.description: All dogs must be kept on a leash in common areas, except within designated dog park spaces.

rules.title: Pet Waste Cleanup
rules.description: Owners must promptly pick up and properly dispose of pet waste in all common areas and private yards visible from common areas.

rules.title: Littering in Common Areas
rules.description: Discarding trash, cigarette butts, or other debris in common areas is prohibited.

rules.title: Short-Term Rental Prohibition
rules.description: Rentals of less than 30 days, including through platforms such as Airbnb or VRBO, are not permitted.

rules.title: Unauthorized Use of Accessible Parking
rules.description: Parking in accessible (disabled) spaces without proper placard or plates is prohibited.

rules.title: Smoking Restrictions
rules.description: Smoking is not permitted within 25 feet of building entrances, playgrounds, or the pool area.

rules.title: Playground Hours and Supervision
rules.description: Playgrounds are open from sunrise to dusk only. Children must be supervised by a responsible adult.

rules.title: Construction Work Hours
rules.description: Exterior construction and loud maintenance work is allowed only between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM on weekdays and 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM on Saturdays. No work on Sundays or holidays.

amenity_reservations.event_description: Resident birthday celebration with light refreshments and music.
Baby shower for friends and neighbors.
Community board-hosted Q&A session with light snacks.
Neighborhood game night (event canceled due to low RSVP).

architectural_requests.description: Repaint entire exterior stucco and trim using the pre-approved Neutral Beige color, with Crisp White Trim on fascia and window frames.
architectural_requests.decision_notes: Approved as submitted. Work hours must comply with construction noise rules. Notify neighbors 48 hours before work begins.

architectural_requests.description: Replace front yard turf with drought-tolerant plants and gravel ground cover, maintaining required sight lines and tree setbacks.
Request to repaint exterior stucco using Neutral Sandstone with Soft Cream Trim on columns and window surrounds.
Install recessed lighting and run new wiring in the living room ceiling.
architectural_requests.decision_notes: Denied due to incomplete contractor licensing information. Resubmit with required documentation.

architectural_requests.description: Change front door color to Heritage Red and repaint trim using Urban Charcoal.
architectural_requests.decision_notes: Owner withdrew request after deciding to keep existing color scheme.

candidates.name: Alex Martinez
candidates.biography: Alex is a software engineer who moved into the community in 2023 and volunteers regularly for community clean-up days.
candidates.platform: Improve communication by enhancing the HOA website, publish clearer financial summaries, and support more family-friendly events.

candidates.name: Priya Desai
candidates.biography: Priya is a financial analyst and has served on the Budget Committee for the past two years.
candidates.platform: Maintain stable dues by prioritizing long-term reserve planning and competitive vendor bidding.

candidates.name: Michael Chen
candidates.biography: Michael is a retired civil engineer who has lived in the community since 2014 and previously chaired the Architectural Review Committee.
candidates.platform: Focus on infrastructure upkeep, consistent enforcement of architectural standards, and transparent project timelines.

candidates.name: Danielle Brooks
candidates.biography: Danielle works in community outreach for a local nonprofit and organizes several social gatherings each year.
candidates.platform: Strengthen community engagement with more inclusive events and better support for new residents.

candidates.name: Samuel Rivera
candidates.biography: Samuel is a small business owner and long-time volunteer for the neighborhood watch program.
candidates.platform: Enhance safety initiatives, improve lighting in common areas, and modernize parking and guest permit policies.

candidates.name: Patricia O’Neil
candidates.biography: Patricia is a retired school principal who has served on the Board for two prior terms and helped oversee the clubhouse renovation.
candidates.platform: Preserve property values through proactive maintenance, fair rule enforcement, and responsive management oversight.

candidates.name: Jordan Lee
candidates.biography: Jordan is an architect who enjoys using their expertise to review design proposals and exterior changes.
candidates.platform: Streamline the architectural review process while maintaining consistent standards across the community.

candidates.name: Renee Thompson
candidates.biography: Renee is a project manager with experience in vendor selection and contract negotiations.
candidates.platform: Negotiate better contracts with landscaping and maintenance providers to reduce long-term costs.

candidates.name: Carlos Gutierrez
candidates.biography: Carlos is a recent resident and active participant in the community forum and social events.
candidates.platform: Increase transparency in decision-making and involve more residents through surveys and town halls.

candidates.name: Emily Rogers
candidates.biography: Emily has chaired the Social Committee and helped launch several successful community traditions.
candidates.platform: Balance enforcement with education by providing clearer guidelines and reminders before issuing fines.

events.title: Winter Neighbor Potluck
events.description: Bring a dish to share and meet your neighbors. Beverages and paper goods provided by the HOA.

events.title: Saturday Morning Yoga
events.description: All-levels yoga session. Bring your own mat and water bottle.

events.title: Board Q&A and Coffee
events.description: Informal question-and-answer session with Board members about upcoming projects and community priorities.

events.title: Food Truck Sunday
events.description: Rotating local food trucks with options for all ages. Limited seating availablebring chairs or blankets.

events.title: Spring Community Clean-Up Day
events.description: Join neighbors to pick up litter and refresh common areas. Gloves and bags provided.
events.location: Meet at North Playground near Building B

events.title: Regular HOA Board Meeting
events.description: Monthly Board meeting. Open forum for resident comments in the first 30 minutes.

events.title: Family Movie Night
events.description: Outdoor family-friendly movie with free popcorn. Bring blankets or low chairs.

events.title: Teen Game Tournament
events.description: Video and board game tournament for residents ages 131. Snacks and prizes provided.

events.title: Spring Egg Hunt
events.description: Annual egg hunt with separate areas for younger and older children. Check-in starts 30 minutes early.

events.title: Wine & Cheese Social
events.description: Adult social mixer featuring a selection of wines and non-alcoholic beverages. Ages 21+ only.
events.location: Lakeside Clubhouse Great Room and Patio

events.title: Board Q&A: Capital Projects
events.description: Detailed discussion of upcoming capital projects including exterior painting and pool deck repairs.

events.title: Annual Membership Meeting
events.description: Yearly meeting to review financials, hear committee reports, and announce election results.

events.title: Pool Opening Party
events.description: Celebrate the start of pool season with music, games, and refreshments.

event_registrations.notes: Unable to attend due to travel.
Wants to ask about exterior paint schedule.
Planning to speak during open forum.
Okay with standing room if space opens.

invoices.label: Quarter 3 2024
invoices.notes: Paid five days after due date; separate late fee invoice issued.

invoices.label: Late Fee - Quarter 3 2024
invoices.notes: Late fee for Q3 2024 payment received after grace period.

invoices.label: Quarter 3 2024 - Pool Deck Special Assessment
invoices.notes: One-time special assessment for pool deck resurfacing project.

invoices.label: Quarter 2 2024 - Billing Adjustment
invoices.notes: Test adjustment entry; canceled and replaced by corrected credit.

invoices.notes: One-time special assessment to replenish roof replacement reserves.

invoices.notes: Current quarter HOA dues. Eligible for online payment using available methods.

forum_topics.subject: Lost house key near main entrance
forum_topics.body: I lost a single silver house key near the main entrance gate around 7 PM this evening. It is on a small black rubber key ring with no other keys attached. If anyone spots it near the visitor parking or pedestrian gate, please let me know.

forum_topics.subject: Found dog tag by north playground
forum_topics.body: I found a round brass dog tag near the north playground by Building B earlier today. It has a paw print on one side and some worn engraving on the other but no readable phone number. Message me if you are missing one and can describe it.

forum_topics.subject: Question about extended pool hours in summer
forum_topics.body: Does the HOA ever extend pool hours later into the evening during the summer months? A few neighbors mentioned that last year the pool stayed open until 11 PM on Fridays, but I dont see that noted anywhere in the current rules.

forum_topics.subject: Early morning trash truck noise
forum_topics.body: Has anyone else noticed that the trash trucks have been arriving before 6 AM lately? It seems to be happening at least twice a week and is very loud under the building B windows.

forum_topics.subject: Carpooling and dishes for Winter Neighbor Potluck
forum_topics.body: For the upcoming Winter Neighbor Potluck at the clubhouse, is anyone interested in carpooling from the far west side near Building F? Also, it might help to list what dish you are bringing so we dont end up with all desserts.

forum_topics.subject: North playground swing safety concern
forum_topics.body: One of the swings at the north playground near Building B feels loose and makes a creaking sound when kids are using it. I submitted a maintenance request, but wanted to flag it here as well so parents are aware until its repaired.

forum_posts.body: I lost a single silver house key near the main entrance gate around 7 PM this evening. Its on a small black rubber key ring with no other keys attached. I walked from the visitor parking up to the pedestrian gate and only noticed it was missing when I got back to my unit. If anyone spots it along that route, please let me know.
If found, please leave it with the front desk or send me a direct message here so I can arrange to pick it up. Thank you!

I found a round brass dog tag near the north playground by Building B earlier today. It has a paw print on one side and some worn engraving on the other, but I cant make out the phone number. I turned it in to the office, but if you are missing a tag like this and can describe the engraving, Im happy to help you reclaim it.
Thank you for turning it in. I remember seeing a flyer about a missing collar in Building C. Ill point them to this thread and the office in case its theirs.

Does the HOA ever extend pool hours later into the evening during the summer months? A few neighbors mentioned that last year the pool stayed open until 11 PM on Fridays, but I dont see that noted anywhere in the current rules or on the pool sign. Wondering if that will happen again this year.

Last summer the Board approved extended hours on Fridays and Saturdays only, but it was a trial run. They mentioned at the January meeting that theyll decide in April whether to do it again, so it might depend on the upcoming Board Q&A and resident feedback.

Has anyone else noticed that the trash trucks have been arriving before 6 AM lately? It has happened at least twice a week for the past month, and its extremely loud under the Building B windows. Im not sure if the vendor changed their schedule, but it seems earlier than what the rules allow for construction and service noise.

Ive noticed it too by Building A. At the last Board meeting they mentioned they were contacting the waste company about sticking to a later window. It might help if a few more residents email the office so they can log additional complaints.

For the upcoming Winter Neighbor Potluck at the clubhouse, is anyone interested in carpooling from the far west side near Building F? Parking gets tight around 6 PM. I can take two extra people. Also, it might help if we list what were bringing so we dont end up with all desserts. Im planning to bring a quinoa salad.
Id love to carpool from Building F and can be ready by 5:45 PM. Ill bring a tray of roasted vegetables so we have another main dish option.

One of the swings at the north playground near Building B feels loose and makes a loud creaking sound when kids are using it. I submitted a maintenance request so the HOA is aware, but I wanted to flag it here as well. Parents may want to steer kids to the other swings until its inspected and fixed.

Thanks for posting this. I saw the maintenance crew out there this morning looking at the swing set, so hopefully it will be repaired soon. Until then were avoiding that particular swing as you suggested.
